root_cause_explanation: The flakiness risk came from a data race in concurrent statistics building: shared fields (e.g., table row count) could be read/updated concurrently by multiple goroutines during column/index histogram building. This PR stabilizes it by using atomic operations when accessing/updating the shared counter and by restructuring the build to run column builds concurrently without unsynchronized shared-state mutation.
fix_pattern: Synchronize concurrent statistics building to avoid races on shared counters/state (e.g., use atomic Load/Store for shared table statistics fields updated across goroutines).

evidence: files: plan/statistics/statistics.go, plan/statistics/statistics_test.go, executor/analyze.go, sessionctx/variable/session.go, sessionctx/variable/sysvar.go, sessionctx/varsutil/varsutil.go
